theorem: Triadic keystone: 2^k Rule 90 rows hold exactly 3^k live cells (Sierpinski 3-fold); Lucas is already a 3-fold generalization of Rule 30; the triad recurs at every bound stage
operator_thesis: every forge literalizes one stage of one proof and applies the same logic; the unison plus the triadic recursion is the key to the whole being true
keystone: 2^k Rule 90 rows contain exactly 3^k live cells (Sierpinski 3-fold); this is why Lucas is O(log N) and why the same triad recurs at every level below

triadic_census.triality_S3 (p03): S3 = Weyl(A2), 3 transpositions; D4 triality is 3-fold
triadic_census.SU3_closure_T4 (p03): the n=3 conditional matrix closes in exactly 3 steps
triadic_census.three_move_closure: the bounded repair window is exactly 3 moves (T4)

problem_3_sub_O_N.framework_answer: O(log N) readout in the streaming aggregate-during-enumeration model
problem_3_sub_O_N.verified_fact: ReadoutForge reads bit N in log2(N)+1 ops, bit-exact (paper 10)
problem_3_sub_O_N.epistemic_status: readout O(log N) verified; cold extraction with no enumeration remains open

problem_2_equal_density.framework_answer: 4/4 flip-preserve split + uniform F4 invariant measure on the trace-2 stratum
problem_2_equal_density.verified_fact: EntropyForge 4 flip / 4 preserve; T3 isomorphism uniform stratum
problem_2_equal_density.epistemic_status: local split exact; asymptotic density by transport, not independent proof

problem_1_non_periodicity.framework_answer: unbounded 3^k growth + SU(3) orbit non-closure on the trace-2 stratum
problem_1_non_periodicity.verified_fact: 3^k-in-2^k law exact to k=11; finite non-periodicity window (no period <=256 in 2048 bits, EntropyForge)
problem_1_non_periodicity.epistemic_status: finite window verified; unbounded statement by transport, not proved

honesty: This binds the VERIFIED triadic facts and records the framework's structural transport arguments for the three Wolfram problems WITH their epistemic status. It does NOT claim the famous problems are closed in the literature.
